$schema: Manifest ↔ Prisma schema-drift allowlist. See scripts/manifest/audit-schema-drift.mjs for semantics.

_doc.globalAdapterDerived: Cross-cutting fields that EVERY Prisma store adapter injects from runtime context, not from command input. Applies to every governed entity.
_doc.adapterDerived: Per-entity fields that the entity's Prisma store adapter derives via a documented rule (e.g. looking up a parent entity, normalizing case from manifest camelCase to Prisma snake_case).
_doc.nonconforming: Per constitution §14, acknowledged tech debt with an active removal plan. Bypass is not permission.
_doc.ignoredEntities: Entity names to skip entirely (e.g. infrastructure tables incorrectly tagged as governed).
_doc.ignoredFields: Per-entity field allowlist for fields the audit should not enforce.

tenantId.rule: Every Prisma store adapter injects tenantId from RuntimeContext.user.tenantId. See packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-store.ts where each .create() sets `tenantId: this.tenantId`. Constitution §19 requires runtime tenant context on every governed command.

first_name.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `first_name` (no @map); manifest declares camelCase `firstName`. ClientContactPrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch04-client-trio.ts normalizes via `first_name: asString(data.first_name ?? data.firstName)`. Manifest-driven flow always passes `firstName`. Tech-debt note: the cleaner long-term fix is renaming the Prisma field to `firstName @map("first_name")` so the schema convention is uniform; deferred to avoid a Prisma-first change in violation of constitution §14 ordering.

last_name.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `last_name` (no @map); manifest declares camelCase `lastName`. Same adapter handling as `first_name` in ClientContactPrismaStore (broken-read-batch04-client-trio.ts).

board_name.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `board_name` (no @map); manifest declares `boardName`. BattleBoardPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch02) normalizes via `board_name: asString(data.board_name ?? data.boardName)`.

customer_id.rule: Prisma field is `customer_id @db.Uuid` (no @map); manifest declares `customerId`. CateringOrderPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch03) normalizes via `customer_id: asString(data.customer_id ?? data.customerId)`.

delivery_date.rule: Prisma field is `delivery_date DateTime` (no @map); manifest declares `deliveryDate: number`. Adapter normalizes via `data.delivery_date ?? data.deliveryDate` and converts to Date.

delivery_time.rule: Prisma field is `delivery_time String` (no @map); manifest declares `deliveryTime`. Adapter normalizes via `data.delivery_time ?? data.deliveryTime`.

supplier_number.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `supplier_number` (no @map); manifest declares `supplierNumber`. InventorySupplierPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch09) normalizes via `supplier_number: asString(data.supplierNumber ?? data.supplier_number)`.

trigger_type.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `trigger_type` (no @map); manifest declares `triggerType`. WorkflowPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch01) normalizes via `trigger_type: asString(data.trigger_type ?? data.triggerType)`.

recipient_employee_id.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `recipient_employee_id @db.Uuid` (no @map); manifest declares `recipientEmployeeId`. NotificationPrismaStore.create reads `data.recipientEmployeeId` and writes to `recipient_employee_id`. One-direction (manifest→Prisma) is correct for the dispatcher path; raw snake_case input is not supported and not needed because all governed flows go through the manifest dispatcher.

notification_type.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `notification_type` (no @map); manifest declares `notificationType`. Same one-direction adapter pattern as `recipient_employee_id`.

schedule_date.rule: Prisma field is `schedule_date DateTime @db.Date` (no @map); manifest declares `scheduleDate: number`. SchedulePrismaStore.create reads `data.scheduleDate` and writes to `schedule_date` (one-direction; manifest dispatcher only).

shift_start.rule: Prisma field is `shift_start DateTime @db.Timestamptz(6)` (no @map); manifest declares `shiftStart: number`. ScheduleShiftPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch13) normalizes via `data.shift_start ?? data.shiftStart` and converts to Date.

shift_end.rule: Prisma field is `shift_end DateTime @db.Timestamptz(6)` (no @map); manifest declares `shiftEnd: number`. Same adapter handling as `shift_start` in ScheduleShiftPrismaStore.
